description: Following the 2018 report by the Coal Commission, Germany debated the timeline for phasing out coal-fired power plants to meet climate targets. The process involved intense negotiations between the government, environmental NGOs, and industrial lobbyists, culminating in the 2020 Coal Phase-out Act which targeted a full exit by 2038.

key_events.description: Establishment of the Commission on Growth, Structural Change and Employment (Coal Commission) in June 2018.
The Coal Commission presents its final report in January 2019, recommending a 2038 phase-out date.
Intense lobbying by coal-dependent states in Lusatia and Rhenish mining areas causes a dip in support for aggressive timelines.
The Bundestag passes the Coal Phase-out Act in July 2020, codifying the 2038 deadline.

calibration_notes: This scenario demonstrates how public opinion balances high-level climate commitments against localized economic anxiety and job security in industrial regions.
